---
title: "selscan"
author: "Andrea Estandia"
date: "07/04/2026"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
# Knitr settings: 
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file())
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE)
options(scipen = 999)
```

```{r}
source("./src/wm-europe_source.R")
```

```{r}
pops <- c("uk", "shetland", "norway", "spain")
chrs <- c("chr1", "chr2")

nsl_all <- expand.grid(pop = pops, chr = chrs, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  mutate(
    file = file.path(
      reports_path,
      "selection",
      pop,
      "nsl",
      chr,
      paste0(pop, "_", chr, "_nsl.nsl.out")
    ),
    data = map(file, \(f) {
      if (!file.exists(f)) return(NULL)
      fread(f, header = TRUE) %>%
        dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(c("pop", "chr")))
    })
  ) %>%
  select(pop, chr, data) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(data)


nsl_all <-
nsl_all %>%
  filter(nsl > 0.5) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = pos, y = nsl,col=chr)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.6, alpha = 0.6) +
  facet_grid(pop ~ chr) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Position",
    y = "nSL",
    title = "nSL values across chr1 and chr2 for each population"
  )

ggsave(
 nsl_all,
  filename=file.path(figures_path, "selection", "nsl_all.png"),
  width = 10,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r}
xpehh_path   <- file.path(reports_path, "selection", "xpehh")

pair_dirs <- list.dirs(xpehh_path, recursive = FALSE, full.names = TRUE)

first_or_na <- function(x) {
  if (length(x) == 0) NA_character_ else x[1]
}

read_one_out <- function(out_file, chr_name, pair_name) {
  dt <- fread(out_file)
  nms <- names(dt)

  pos_col <- first_or_na(nms[grepl("pos|bp|base", nms, ignore.case = TRUE)])
  if (is.na(pos_col)) pos_col <- nms[2]

  score_col <- first_or_na(nms[grepl("xpehh|xp_ehh|score|stat|value|val", nms, ignore.case = TRUE)])
  if (is.na(score_col)) score_col <- nms[length(nms)]

  dt <- dt[, .(
    pair = pair_name,
    chr  = chr_name,
    pos  = as.numeric(get(pos_col)),
    stat = as.numeric(get(score_col))
  )]

  dt[!is.na(pos) & !is.na(stat)]
}

plot_pair <- function(pair_dir) {
  pair_name <- basename(pair_dir)
  chr_dirs <- list.dirs(pair_dir, recursive = FALSE, full.names = TRUE)

  all_chr <- rbindlist(lapply(chr_dirs, function(chr_dir) {
    out_file <- list.files(chr_dir, pattern = "\\.xpehh\\.out$", full.names = TRUE)
    if (length(out_file) == 0) return(NULL)

    chr_name <- basename(chr_dir)
    read_one_out(out_file[1], chr_name, pair_name)
  }), fill = TRUE)

  if (nrow(all_chr) == 0) return(NULL)

  # Keep chr1 then chr2 in order
  all_chr[, chr := factor(chr, levels = c("chr1", "chr2"))]
  setorder(all_chr, chr, pos)

  # Build a single continuous x axis: chr2 follows chr1
  chr_info <- all_chr[, .(
    chr_len = max(pos, na.rm = TRUE)
  ), by = chr][order(chr)]

  chr_info[, offset := shift(cumsum(chr_len), fill = 0)]
  chr_info[, center := offset + chr_len / 2]

  all_chr <- merge(all_chr, chr_info[, .(chr, offset)], by = "chr", all.x = TRUE)
  all_chr[, x_plot := pos + offset]

  # thresholds
  mu <- mean(all_chr$stat, na.rm = TRUE)
  sdv <- sd(all_chr$stat, na.rm = TRUE)
  upper <- mu + 3 * sdv
  lower <- mu - 3 * sdv

  ggplot(all_chr, aes(x = x_plot, y = stat, col = chr)) +
    geom_point(size = 0.35, alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = upper, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = lower, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("chr1" = "#2C3E50", "chr2" = "#95A5A6")) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = chr_info$center,
      labels = gsub("chr|scaffold", "", chr_info$chr),
      expand = c(0.01, 0.01)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste0(pair_name, " (±3 SD thresholds)"),
      x = "\nChromosome",
      y = "XPEHH\n"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
      axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"),
      text = element_text(family = "roboto_condensed", size = 16),
      legend.position = "none",
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    )
}

plots <- lapply(pair_dirs, plot_pair)
names(plots) <- basename(pair_dirs)

plot_dir <- file.path(figures_path, "xpehh")
dir.create(plot_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

for (nm in names(plots)) {
  if (is.null(plots[[nm]])) next

  ggsave(
    filename = file.path(plot_dir, paste0(nm, "_xpehh_chr1_chr2.png")),
    plot = plots[[nm]],
    width = 12,
    height = 4,
    dpi = 300
  )
}

```

```{r}
all_data <- rbindlist(lapply(pair_dirs, function(pair_dir) {
  pair_name <- basename(pair_dir)
  chr_dirs <- list.dirs(pair_dir, recursive = FALSE, full.names = TRUE)

  rbindlist(lapply(chr_dirs, function(chr_dir) {
    out_file <- list.files(chr_dir, pattern = "\\.xpehh\\.out$", full.names = TRUE)
    if (length(out_file) == 0) return(NULL)

    chr_name <- basename(chr_dir)
    read_one_out(out_file[1], chr_name, pair_name)
  }), fill = TRUE)
}), fill = TRUE)

all_data[, c("pop1", "pop2") := tstrsplit(pair, "_vs_")]

all_data[, stat_std := as.numeric(scale(stat)), by = pair]

# all_data already created with:
# pair, chr, pos, stat
# and:
# all_data[, c("pop1", "pop2") := tstrsplit(pair, "_vs_")]
# all_data[, stat_std := as.numeric(scale(stat)), by = pair]

window_size <- 1e5   # 100 kb
threshold   <- 2     # after orientation, use positive threshold
min_pairs   <- 2     # region must appear in at least this many pairwise comparisons

find_common_regions_for_pop <- function(all_data, target_pop,
                                        window_size = 1e5,
                                        threshold = 2,
                                        min_pairs = 2) {
  dt <- all_data[pop1 == target_pop | pop2 == target_pop]
  if (nrow(dt) == 0) return(NULL)

  # Orient so positive always means selection in target_pop
  dt[, sel_stat := fifelse(pop1 == target_pop, stat_std, -stat_std)]

  # Bin into windows
  dt[, window := floor(pos / window_size)]

  # Summarize by pair/chromosome/window
  window_stats <- dt[, .(
    mean_sel = mean(sel_stat, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_sel  = max(sel_stat, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_sel  = min(sel_stat, na.rm = TRUE)
  ), by = .(pair, chr, window)]

  # Keep windows with strong positive signal in target population
  selected_windows <- window_stats[max_sel >= threshold]

  if (nrow(selected_windows) == 0) return(NULL)

  # Count how many comparisons support each region
  common_regions <- selected_windows[, .N, by = .(chr, window)]
  common_regions <- common_regions[N >= min_pairs]

  if (nrow(common_regions) == 0) return(NULL)

  common_regions[, `:=`(
    pop = target_pop,
    start = window * window_size,
    end   = (window + 1) * window_size
  )]

  # Add supporting pair count and mean signal
  support <- selected_windows[, .(
    n_pairs = uniqueN(pair),
    strongest = max(max_sel, na.rm = TRUE)
  ), by = .(chr, window)]

  common_regions <- merge(common_regions, support, by = c("chr", "window"), all.x = TRUE)
  common_regions[order(chr, start)]
}

# Run for all populations
pops <- sort(unique(c(all_data$pop1, all_data$pop2)))

regions_by_pop <- lapply(pops, function(p) {
  find_common_regions_for_pop(
    all_data = all_data,
    target_pop = p,
    window_size = window_size,
    threshold = threshold,
    min_pairs = min_pairs
  )
})

names(regions_by_pop) <- pops

# Combine into one table
common_regions_all <- rbindlist(regions_by_pop, fill = TRUE)

common_regions_all


# keep only supported windows
hits <- common_regions_all[n_pairs >= 2][order(pop, chr, start)]

# merge adjacent 100 kb windows into continuous regions
hits[, gap := start - shift(end, fill = first(start)), by = .(pop, chr)]
hits[, region_id := cumsum(is.na(gap) | gap > 0), by = .(pop, chr)]

regions <- hits[, .(
  region_start = min(start),
  region_end   = max(end),
  n_windows    = .N,
  n_pairs_max  = max(n_pairs, na.rm = TRUE),
  n_pairs_mean = mean(n_pairs, na.rm = TRUE),
  strongest    = max(strongest, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = .(pop, chr, region_id)]

regions[, region_size_kb := (region_end - region_start) / 1000]
regions <- regions[order(pop, chr, region_start)]
regions

top_regions <- regions[order(pop, -n_pairs_max, -strongest, -region_size_kb)]
top_regions

summary_by_pop <- regions[, .(
  n_regions = .N,
  total_kb  = sum(region_size_kb, na.rm = TRUE),
  best_chr  = chr[which.max(strongest)],
  best_start = region_start[which.max(strongest)],
  best_end   = region_end[which.max(strongest)],
  best_strongest = max(strongest, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = pop]

summary_by_pop

top5_by_pop <- regions[, head(.SD[order(-n_pairs_max, -strongest)], 5), by = pop]
top5_by_pop

filtered_regions <- regions[
  n_pairs_max >= 3 &          # consistent across all comparisons
  strongest >= 5 &            # strong signal
  region_size_kb <= 5000      # <= 5 Mb (important!)
]

filtered_regions[order(pop, -strongest)]

merge_gap <- 1e6  # 1 Mb gap allowed

setorder(filtered_regions, pop, chr, region_start)

filtered_regions[, gap := region_start - shift(region_end), by = .(pop, chr)]
filtered_regions[, group := cumsum(is.na(gap) | gap > merge_gap), by = .(pop, chr)]

merged_regions <- filtered_regions[, .(
  region_start = min(region_start),
  region_end   = max(region_end),
  strongest    = max(strongest),
  n_regions    = .N
), by = .(pop, chr, group)]

merged_regions[, size_kb := (region_end - region_start)/1000]
merged_regions[order(pop, chr, region_start)]
```
